title: Area Guide
intro: The cabin sits between Lake Cushman and Lake Kokanee, at the doorstep of Olympic National Park. Golf, hiking, swimming, disc golf, and the town of Hoodsport are all within a few minutes — here's what's nearby.

featured.name: LCMC Park & Boat Launch
featured.description: Just a quick walk from the front door: dock, boat launch, children's swimming area, picnic tables, and a lake fishing area. One guest parking pass is included with your stay.
featured.imageAlt: Long dock stretching into Lake Cushman at the community park

attractions.name: Lake Cushman Golf Course
attractions.description: A challenge for all levels — low handicappers can hone their skills in a no-pressure environment while high handicappers will have their hands full. Meticulously maintained by the Lake Cushman Maintenance Company. 9 holes, par 72 (18 played), driving range, clubhouse, and a covered gazebo with cooking facilities.
attractions.imageAlt: Course map of Lake Cushman Golf Course

attractions.name: Hoodsport Community Trail Park
attractions.description: Short trails off Highway 119 weaving back and forth across Dow Creek — a half-mile loop and a slightly steeper 1.5-mile loop, friendly to most hikers and built by local 4-H club members. Picnic tables and a restroom at the trailhead.
attractions.imageAlt: Trail map for the Hoodsport Trail

attractions.name: Hoodsport Hills Disc Golf Course
attractions.description: A family-friendly 9-hole disc golf course across Hwy 119 from the Trail Park — concrete pads, basket targets, moderately hilly and heavily wooded. Parking on both sides of the highway, picnic tables, restroom, and a gazebo.
attractions.imageAlt: Player throwing a disc from a concrete tee pad in the woods

attractions.name: Lake Cushman Dam Viewpoint
attractions.description: View Cushman Dam No. 1 — dedicated in 1926 when President Coolidge pressed a button in the White House — and the lake it holds back. Drivable by vehicle with plenty of parking.
attractions.imageAlt: Welcome sign at the Cushman No. 1 Dam viewpoint

attractions.name: Lake Cushman Lookout
attractions.description: A beautiful scenic viewpoint just off North Lake Cushman Road, with a gravel parking area, info boards, and a restroom. Well worth the stop on your way to the north end of the lake.
attractions.imageAlt: Panoramic view over Lake Cushman with snow-capped Olympic peaks

attractions.name: Skokomish Park at Lake Cushman
attractions.description: Over 500 acres with three boat launches on Lake Cushman's freshwater shoreline. A fantastic day-use area with picnic sites, a shelter, and lawn games, plus 60+ campsites. Formerly Lake Cushman State Park.
attractions.imageAlt: Skokomish Park at Lake Cushman entrance sign

attractions.name: Bear Gulch Picnic Site
attractions.description: A popular picnic area beside the scenic North Fork Skokomish River on the north side of the lake. Picnic tables and a vault toilet, with a rocky beach for swimming and wading. On Forest Road 24 just south of Staircase.

attractions.name: Copper Creek Trailhead
attractions.description: A steep 2.1-mile trail forming a short loop at the ridge top, passing the Brown Mule mine site and the abandoned Apex Mine along Copper Creek canyon. The ridge leads toward Timber Mountain and Lightning Peak.

attractions.name: Shady Lane Trail
attractions.description: A favorite year-round family hike. Pass tall cedars, giant firs, and hemlock along the North Fork Skokomish — about a mile to the Staircase Ranger Station, and four miles round-trip if you continue to the rapids. Watch for elk.
attractions.imageAlt: Moss-covered old-growth forest along the Shady Lane trail

attractions.name: North Fork Skokomish Trailhead
attractions.description: Day-hike the valley portion of this 15.1-mile trail that eventually reaches the Duckabush River. Planning an overnight? Check in with the Wilderness Information Center first.

attractions.name: Skokomish River Suspension Bridge
attractions.description: On the Staircase Rapids Trail — a $1.2-million suspension bridge completed in 2013 after the original washed out in 1998. Enjoy the long, terraced rapids from several perspectives, including from the bridge itself.
attractions.imageAlt: Suspension bridge over the North Fork Skokomish River

attractions.name: Staircase Rapids Loop
attractions.description: A short, beautiful 2-mile loop from the Staircase Ranger Station through quintessential Olympic forest — western redcedar, hemlock, and Douglas fir over lush sword ferns. Mostly level and great for families; continue toward Flapjack Lakes if you want more.
attractions.imageAlt: Staircase Rapids Trail map, Olympic National Park

attractions.name: Mt Ellinor Trail
attractions.description: Rated 5 stars by many. The lower trail climbs gently through forest for the first mile, then gets steeper and rougher toward timberline. Upper and lower trailheads via Forest Road 2419 — the summit rewards with sweeping Olympic views (and often mountain goats).
attractions.imageAlt: Mount Ellinor trail map, Olympic National Forest

attractions.name: Mt Rose Trail
attractions.description: A serious workout: from Hoodsport, take WA-119 about 10 miles to Forest Road 24, then 2 miles to the trailhead. Steep switchbacks to a summit loop with views over Lake Cushman.

attractions.name: Big Creek Loop Trail
attractions.description: A four-mile loop around Big Creek Basin through mature second growth, with benches at creek crossings for lunch in beautiful surroundings. Connects to the Mt Ellinor trails for a 6.2-mile historic climbing route to the summit.
attractions.imageAlt: Big Creek Campground loop trail map

attractions.name: Playland Park
attractions.description: A children's playground in Division 18 of the Lake Cushman community — a quick hop from the cabin for burning off little-kid energy.
attractions.imageAlt: Colorful playground structure among evergreen trees

attractions.name: Division 1 Park
attractions.description: The big community park: covered gazebo with BBQs, dock, boat launch, children's and adult swimming areas, volleyball, basketball, playground equipment, and restrooms with showers. Park hosts on site Memorial Day through Labor Day.
attractions.imageAlt: Sandy beach and swimming area at the Division 1 community park

attractions.name: Division 3 Park
attractions.description: Covered gazebo with BBQs, dock, boat launch, swimming areas, volleyball, basketball, horseshoe court, merry-go-round, and restrooms with showers. Park hosts Memorial Day through Labor Day.

attractions.name: Division 14 Park
attractions.description: The park at the end of the road: dock, boat launch, children's swimming area, picnic tables, and a lake fishing area behind the parking lot. This is the one you can see from the porch.
attractions.imageAlt: Dock and boat launch at the Division 14 park on Lake Cushman
